name: Russian Folk Magic
description: Russian Folk Magic, deeply intertwined with Slavic paganism and Orthodox Christianity, represents a rich tapestry of beliefs and practices passed down through generations. Rooted in rural life and a profound connection to nature, it encompasses a wide range of rituals, spells, and charms designed to influence fate, heal ailments, protect against misfortune, and ensure prosperity. This magic often involves appeasing or manipulating spirits of nature (leshy, vodyanoy, rusalka), household spirits (domovoy), and the spirits of ancestors. Methods include the use of incantations (zagovory), amulets (oberegi), herbal remedies, and symbolic actions. Divination through cards, mirrors, or dreams is also common. The cultural significance is immense, providing a framework for understanding the world, coping with adversity, and maintaining social harmony. It emphasizes the power of words, intention, and the interconnectedness of all things. The practice blends pre-Christian beliefs with Orthodox rituals, resulting in a unique syncretic system where saints are sometimes invoked alongside pagan deities. The core tenets revolve around respecting the natural world, honoring ancestors, and maintaining a balance between the visible and invisible realms. Despite modernization, aspects of Russian Folk Magic persist in contemporary society, particularly in rural areas, where it continues to offer solace, guidance, and a sense of connection to the past.
subtitle: Magic of the Russian Soul

keyMyths.title: The Domovoy's Wrath
keyMyths.description: A family neglects to honor their Domovoy, the household spirit. In response, the Domovoy causes mischief and misfortune until proper offerings are made and respect is restored, demonstrating the importance of honoring household spirits.
keyMyths.source: Folk tales passed down through generations

sources.author: Linda Ivanits
sources.description: Explores various aspects of Russian folk beliefs, including magic, superstitions, and customs.
sources.text: Explores various aspects of Russian folk beliefs, including magic, superstitions, and customs.
